context: Tests whether the agent computes product affinity using the correct lift formula with confidence, applies the right minimum support threshold, excludes bulk orders, and schedules the job correctly.

checklist.name: Lift formula
checklist.description: Affinity score is computed as lift = P(A∩B) / (P(A) * P(B)) — the code must divide the joint probability by the product of individual probabilities

checklist.name: Confidence computed
checklist.description: Confidence P(B|A) = coCount / countA (or pAB / pA) is also computed and stored alongside lift

checklist.name: Minimum support threshold
checklist.description: Pairs with fewer than 3 co-purchases are excluded from affinity records (coCount >= 3 or equivalent)

checklist.name: Bulk order exclusion
checklist.description: Orders containing more than 15 line items are excluded from the co-purchase computation

checklist.name: Bidirectional pairs stored
checklist.description: For each product pair (A, B), both (A→B) and (B→A) affinity records are written so either product can be used as the source lookup key

checklist.name: Upsert on duplicate
checklist.description: Existing affinity records are upserted (updated) rather than duplicated — uses updateOnDuplicate or equivalent ON CONFLICT / upsert logic

checklist.name: Nightly schedule
checklist.description: design-notes.md states the job runs nightly (e.g. via cron, scheduled job, or task scheduler) — not ad-hoc or on every request

checklist.name: Only completed orders
checklist.description: The query filters for completed orders only (status = 'completed' or equivalent) — pending/cancelled orders are not included

checklist.name: Design notes formula explanation
checklist.description: design-notes.md explains the lift formula (P(A∩B) / P(A)*P(B)) in plain language

checklist.name: Minimum threshold rationale
checklist.description: design-notes.md explains WHY a minimum co-purchase count is required (noise/signal quality) — not just that it is applied
